summary: English essayist and novelist, real name Eric Arthur Blair. Known for 1984, Animal Farm, 'Shooting an Elephant,' 'Politics and the English Language,' and Homage to Catalonia.

works.name: Essays (collected)
works.description: Orwell's essays are the most frequently clued aspect of his work. 'Shooting an Elephant' describes killing an elephant 'solely to avoid looking a fool' while stationed as a colonial police officer in Moulmein, Burma, using a .44 Winchester; 'when the white man turns tyrant it is his own freedom that he destroys.' 'Politics and the English Language' rails against 'dying metaphors,' 'pretentious diction,' 'meaningless words,' 'operators/verbal false limbs'; satirically translates Ecclesiastes into bad modern English; provides 6 rules for writing. 'Inside the Whale' defends Henry Miller's Tropic of Cancer using a biblical metaphor (Jonah). 'Such, Such Were the Joys' describes childhood at St. Cyprian's under 'Sambo.' Other essays: 'Good Bad Books' (Uncle Tom's Cabin), 'The Decline of the English Murder,' 'Lear, Tolstoy, and the Fool,' 'Why I Write' (four motives including 'sheer egoism'), 'The Art of Donald McGill' (dirty postcards), 'Boys' Weeklies,' 'The Lion and the Unicorn' ('a family with the wrong members in control'), 'Notes on Nationalism.' He called Kipling a 'good bad poet.'

clues.clue: 'Shooting an Elephant': kills elephant 'to avoid looking a fool' in Moulmein, Burma with .44 Winchester
examples: 'Solely to avoid looking a fool,' this real-life person uses a .44 Winchester on a beast 'gone must' in Moulmein

clues.clue: 'Politics and the English Language': dying metaphors, pretentious diction, meaningless words; translates Ecclesiastes badly; 6 rules
examples: satirically translated a passage from Ecclesiastes into modern English

clues.clue: 'Inside the Whale': defends Henry Miller's Tropic of Cancer; Jonah metaphor
examples: praised the passive attitude taken by Henry Miller in Tropic of Cancer, analogizing him to Jonah

clues.clue: 'A Hanging': 'Ram! Ram! Ram!' cry; dead man's face showing 'unendurable agony'; dodging a puddle
examples: narrates the 'Ram! Ram! Ram!' cry of a Hindu about to be hanged

clues.clue: 'Such, Such Were the Joys': St. Cyprian's, headmaster 'Sambo'
examples: describes his childhood experiences at St. Cyprian's

works.description: Dystopian novel about Winston Smith in Oceania. Julia sends him a note saying 'I love you.' Winston is betrayed by Mr. Charrington and tortured by O'Brien in the Ministry of Truth with rats in Room 101. Emmanuel Goldstein authored The Theory and Practice of Oligarchical Collectivism. The Tehran Conference inspired dividing the world into Eurasia, Eastasia, and Oceania. Coined doublethink, Newspeak, Big Brother, thoughtcrime. 'Freedom is the freedom to say that two plus two equals four' (echoing Zamyatin's We). 'If you want a vision of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face forever.' Orwell's wife Eileen's poem 'End of the Century, 1984' may have inspired the title. Sandra Newman wrote a feminist retelling.

clues.clue: Winston Smith tortured in Room 101 with rats; betrayed by Mr. Charrington; tortured by O'Brien
examples: the main character is tortured in Room 101 for information about Julia
Mr. Charrington and is taken to Ministry of Truth where is tortured by O'Brien

clues.clue: Goldstein's book The Theory and Practice of Oligarchical Collectivism; Trotsky as model
examples: reads The Theory and Practice of Oligarchical Collectivism
used Leon Trotsky as the model for a character with a 'small, goatee beard'
coining the terms doublethink, Newspeak, and Big Brother

clues.clue: 'boot stamping on a human face forever'
examples: 'if you want a vision of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face forever'

works.name: Animal Farm
works.description: Allegorical novella of the Russian Revolution. An anthem for everyone is replaced with a congratulatory song for one being. Mr. Frederick blows up the restored windmill. Napoleon represents Stalin, Snowball represents Trotsky. The Battle of the Cowshed allegorizes the Russian Civil War. Snowball is blamed for trying to sabotage the windmill. Peter Smollett convinced Jonathan Cape to refuse publication.

clues.clue: Allegorical novella; anthem replaced; Mr. Frederick blows up windmill; Napoleon/Snowball
examples: an anthem meant for everyone is replaced with a congratulatory song for one being
Mr. Frederick blows up a restored windmill

works.name: Homage to Catalonia
works.description: Memoir of fighting for the POUM faction of the Republican army in the Spanish Civil War. Shot through the neck by a sniper. Recounts three days atop the Poliorama theater defending POUM HQ during the May Days in Barcelona. Failed to free Georges Kopp. 'Buttered toast!' shouted through a megaphone. Lamented the tendency to say 'manana.'

clues.clue: Fought for POUM in Spanish Civil War; shot in neck; three days atop Poliorama theater
examples: his time fighting in the Spanish Civil War in Homage to Catalonia
failed to free Georges Kopp after recovering from a sniper wound to the neck

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Keep the Aspidistra Flying: Gordon Comstock works at New Albion advertising, writes 'London Pleasures,' Rosemary Waterlow confesses pregnancy. Burmese Days: John Flory commits suicide. Down and Out in Paris and London: first book, semi-fictionalized memoir. The Road to Wigan Pier: conditions of coal miners in northern England; socialism analysis. Coming Up for Air: George Bowling revisits childhood home of Lower Binfield.

clues.clue: Keep the Aspidistra Flying: Gordon Comstock at New Albion, 'London Pleasures' poem, Rosemary pregnant
examples: Rosemary Waterlow confesses she is pregnant to a man who works at the New Albion advertising agency

clues.clue: The Road to Wigan Pier: coal miners in northern England; socialism analysis
examples: describes the conditions of coal miners and the unemployed in the north of England

clues.clue: Down and Out in Paris and London: first book, semi-fictionalized memoir
examples: the first book published by its author, which recounts how its author lived among beggars

comprehensive_summary:
George Orwell (Eric Arthur Blair) is one of the most clued English authors in quizbowl, primarily through his essays. 'Shooting an Elephant' is the most frequent giveaway, describing his killing of an elephant 'solely to avoid looking a fool' while stationed as a colonial police officer in Moulmein, Burma. 'Politics and the English Language' attacks dying metaphors, pretentious diction, and meaningless words, famously translating Ecclesiastes into deliberately bad modern prose. 'Inside the Whale' defends Henry Miller's Tropic of Cancer using the Jonah metaphor. Other major essays include 'Such, Such Were the Joys' (childhood at St. Cyprian's), 'Good Bad Books,' 'The Decline of the English Murder,' 'Lear, Tolstoy, and the Fool,' and 'Why I Write.'

1984 follows Winston Smith in Oceania, tortured with rats in Room 101 by O'Brien after being betrayed by Mr. Charrington. It coined doublethink, Newspeak, and Big Brother. Animal Farm allegorizes the Russian Revolution with Napoleon and Snowball. Homage to Catalonia recounts his time fighting for POUM in the Spanish Civil War, where he was shot in the neck. Keep the Aspidistra Flying features Gordon Comstock at the New Albion agency writing 'London Pleasures.' The Road to Wigan Pier documents coal miners' conditions. Down and Out in Paris and London is his first book.

cards.front: Author: kills an elephant 'solely to avoid looking a fool' while stationed as a colonial police officer in Moulmein, Burma, using a .44 Winchester
Author: rails against 'dying metaphors,' 'pretentious diction,' and 'meaningless words'; satirically translates Ecclesiastes into bad modern prose; offers 6 rules for writing
Author: defends Henry Miller's Tropic of Cancer using a Jonah metaphor; surveys 1930s literature and writers attracted to communism
Author: narrates the 'Ram! Ram! Ram!' cry of a Hindu about to be hanged; reflects 'never tell me that the dead look peaceful'
Novel: Winston Smith receives a note saying 'I love you' from Julia; tortured with rats in Room 101 by O'Brien; betrayed by Mr. Charrington
Novel: Emmanuel Goldstein authored The Theory and Practice of Oligarchical Collectivism; coined doublethink, Newspeak, and Big Brother
Author: fought for the POUM faction of the Republican army in the Spanish Civil War; shot through the neck by a sniper; spent three days atop the Poliorama theater during the May Days
Novel: Gordon Comstock works at the New Albion advertising agency while writing the poem 'London Pleasures'; Rosemary Waterlow confesses she is pregnant
Author: describes the bleak conditions of coal miners in northern England; second section analyzes why not all people are socialists
cards.back: The Road to Wigan Pier (George Orwell)
